---
title: "Evidence Lower Bound (ELBO)"
toc: true
toc-depth: 3
---

## Definition

::: {.thm-box .thm-definition}
### Definition: Evidence Lower Bound (ELBO)
For a probabilistic model with observed data $\mathbf{x}$, latent variables $\mathbf{z}$, parameters $\boldsymbol\theta$, and a variational approximation $q(\mathbf{z})$, the **Evidence Lower Bound (ELBO)** bounds the log-marginal likelihood $\log p(\mathbf{x} | \boldsymbol\theta)$:

$$
\log p(\mathbf{x} | \boldsymbol\theta) \ge \mathrm{ELBO}(q, \boldsymbol\theta)
$$

It can be decomposed into a reconstruction term and a KL-divergence regularization term:
$$
\mathrm{ELBO}(q, \boldsymbol\theta) = \mathbb{E}_{q(\mathbf{z})}[\log p(\mathbf{x} | \mathbf{z}, \boldsymbol\theta)] - D_{\mathrm{KL}}(q(\mathbf{z}) || p(\mathbf{z} | \boldsymbol\theta))
$$
:::

## Intuition

In many complex generative models with latent variables (like Variational Autoencoders), calculating the true evidence (the probability of the data $p(\mathbf{x})$) is intractable because it requires integrating over all possible latent states $\mathbf{z}$. 

Instead of maximizing the true likelihood, Variational Inference maximizes a lower bound: the **ELBO**. 
By maximizing the ELBO, we implicitly push up the true log-likelihood. 

The decomposition perfectly illustrates the trade-off in VAEs:

::: {.thm-box .thm-problem}
### 1. Reconstruction Term ($\mathbb{E}_{q}[\log p]$)
Encourages the model to reconstruct the data well from the latent representation.
:::

::: {.thm-box .thm-problem}
### 2. Regularization Term ($-D_{\mathrm{KL}}(q || p)$)
Penalizes the approximate posterior $q(\mathbf{z})$ for deviating from the prior $p(\mathbf{z})$, forcing the latent space to remain structured and smooth.

:::

## Practice Problems

::: {.thm-box .thm-problem}
### 1. ELBO Inequality
Prove that $\mathrm{ELBO}(q, \boldsymbol\theta) \le \log p(\mathbf{x} | \boldsymbol\theta)$ for any variational distribution $q(\mathbf{z})$ by using Jensen's inequality or by showing that the difference is a KL divergence.
:::

::: {.thm-box .thm-problem}
### 2. Decomposition
Show algebraically that the ELBO can be decomposed into the reconstruction term and the KL divergence term: $\mathrm{ELBO}(q, \boldsymbol\theta) = \mathbb{E}_{q(\mathbf{z})}[\log p(\mathbf{x} | \mathbf{z}, \boldsymbol\theta)] - D_{\mathrm{KL}}(q(\mathbf{z}) || p(\mathbf{z} | \boldsymbol\theta))$.
:::

::: {.thm-box .thm-problem}
### 3. VAE Regularization
In a VAE, explain how the KL divergence term acts as a regularizer. What happens to the learned representations if the KL term dominates the loss? What happens if it is too small?

:::
